ops.title: Hook registration moved from per-repo settings to captain-hook plugin
ops.body:
# Hook registration is plugin-canonical (capt-hook ‚Č• 9.0.0)

Since capt-hook 9.0.0, the captain-hook **plugin** is the sole registrar of Claude Code hooks. Its static `captain_hook/hooks/hooks.json` wires all twelve lifecycle events globally (`uvx capt-hook run <Event>`, SessionStart async, plus two SessionEnd entries: the async session reviewer `uvx capt-hook review run` and, since 9.2.0, `uvx capt-hook run SessionEnd --async` for pack handlers). **`.claude/settings.json` carries no hook wiring anywhere in the fleet** ‚ÄĒ the repo-bootstrap `settings-base` fragment is hooks-free and a guard test (`plugins/repo-bootstrap/tests/test_guides_fragments.py`) enforces it.

## The control surface is `.claude/hooks/packs.toml`

Per-repo behavior is controlled **only** by pack subscription. An event no pack subscribes to no-ops cleanly (~one `uvx` exec, exit 0). To change what fires in a repo, edit `packs.toml` (or `capt-hook pack add/remove` ‚ÄĒ settings-safe since 9.0.0); never add hook entries to `settings.json` or a guides fragment.

## Why it moved

Claude Code dedupes hooks **per source** (`${pluginRoot ?? ""}\0<command>`, anthropics/claude-code#76297), so identical commands registered from both settings and the plugin fired once *per source*, concurrently (the triplicate-notes incident). And settings.json is a cc-guides-rendered artifact under union-only JSON merge ‚ÄĒ hook keys could be added but never removed, making settings wiring unfixable drift. The plugin is the one source that updates atomically fleet-wide.

## Consequences and gotchas

- **`capt-hook review enable` does two things**: watch the repo + register the plugin. It no longer writes any settings wiring (`ensure_review_wiring` retired in 9.2.0). The session reviewer rides the plugin: a repo where the captain-hook plugin is **not enabled gets no reviewer and no capt-hook hooks at all**.
- **New hook events ship via a capt-hook release** (add to `captain_hook/hooks/hooks.json`), never via settings edits. Repos pick them up next session with zero config changes.
- **Debugging**: check (1) plugin enabled (`enabledPlugins` has `captain-hook@captain-hook`), (2) the pack subscribing to the event is enabled in `packs.toml`, (3) session logs for the dispatch. Multi-fires mean a stale second registration source ‚ÄĒ hunt for leftover settings entries.
- **Local pin / dev launcher escape hatch** (the captain-hook dev repo pattern): set `"captain-hook@captain-hook": false` in the repo's `enabledPlugins` and register a local launcher in `settings.local.json`. That is the only sanctioned way to run a non-uvx dispatcher.
- **Migrating an old repo**: delete every `capt-hook` hook group from `.claude/settings.json` / `settings.local.json`; keep `extraKnownMarketplaces` + `enabledPlugins`. Leftover wiring double-fires decision events (PreToolUse, Stop, SubagentStop, PermissionRequest ‚ÄĒ exempt from the once-guard).

Docs telling this story: repo-bootstrap `reference/hooks.md`, `reference/base-conventions.md`, `SKILL.md` (0.43.1+); captain-hook `docs/guide/philosophy.qmd`.
ops.when: when configuring, debugging, or extending hooks in a scaffolded repo; when migrating existing hook configs
